agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace development servicing bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal the current Burnaby Subdivision Control and Development Servicing Bylaw and replace it with a new Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would update the rules for how new developments connect to city services like water, sewer, and roads. If approved, it would change the requirements and fees for developers and builders working on subdivisions and new construction projects in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw details with your development team or lawyer to understand how new rules could affect your project costs and timelines.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
PROPOSED NEW BURNABY WORKS AND SERVICES BYLAW 2026
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal the Burnaby Subdivision Control and Development Servicing Bylaw and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mid-Block Connection Policy up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Mid-Block Connection Policy. If approved, this policy would create new rules for building pathways that cut through large blocks of land, making it easier for people to walk or bike between streets without going around the block. The policy would apply to new developments and major redevelopments in certain areas of the city. This is not yet law — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if your property could be affected by new mid-block connection requirements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BURNABY MID-BLOCK CONNECTION POLICY
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed Mid-block Connection Policy.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Temporary Use Permit for indoor recreation at 2150 Douglas Road
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Temporary Use Permit (TUP) for a portion of the upper storey at 2150 Douglas Road. A Temporary Use Permit allows a property to be used for a specific purpose for a limited time — in this case, indoor recreation. This is not a permanent change; the permit, if approved, would only allow the recreation use for a set period. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this permit could affect nearby businesses or property use.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
TUP #26-02 - 2150 DOUGLAS ROAD - PORTION OF UPPER STOREY
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Temporary Use Permit (TUP) #26-02 to permit an indoor recreation use at 2150 Douglas Road.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Temporary Use Permit for social services at 7375 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Temporary Use Permit (TUP) for 7375 Kingsway. A TUP is a short-term permission that allows a property to be used for something not normally allowed by the current zoning rules. This permit would allow the site to be used as a social service centre, a place for religious gatherings, and a public assembly space. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit your comments if you are a nearby business owner or resident.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
TUP #26-06 - 7375 KINGSWAY
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Temporary Use Permit (TUP) #26-06 to permit social service centre, religious assembly and public assembly uses at 7375 Kingsway.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Vancouver-SFU cycling route contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for Phase 2 of the Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection. This proposed project would build a new bike route connecting Vancouver to Simon Fraser University. If approved, the contract would hire a company to design and build the cycling path. This could affect businesses near the route due to construction disruptions and potential changes in customer access. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the contract award at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if your business is near the proposed route, consider contacting Council about construction impacts.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - (CB - 7030) VANCOUVER - SFU CYCLING CONNECTION PHASE 2
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Vancouver to SFU Cycling Connection Phase 2 project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on sewer and waterworks contract renewal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract renewal for sewer and waterworks components. This decision would approve a new agreement with the current supplier to continue providing parts and materials needed to maintain and repair the city's underground sewer and water pipes. If approved, the renewal would ensure the city has a steady supply of these essential components for ongoing infrastructure work. The specific terms, cost, and duration of the proposed contract are detailed in the council agenda. Business owners who work on city infrastructure projects or rely on municipal water and sewer services should be aware that this decision could affect the availability and cost of these materials.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect the supply and cost of sewer and water components for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CR - SEWER AND WATERWORK COMPONENTS
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Sewer and Waterworks Components.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to adopt capital project program review recommendations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adopting recommendations from a review of Burnaby's Capital Project Program. The review, done by The Stewart Group, looks at how the city plans and manages big building and infrastructure projects. If approved, the recommendations could change how the city handles future capital projects — which may affect businesses that work on or rely on these projects.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - BURNABY CAPITAL PROJECT PROGRAM REVIEW
Purpose: To adopt recommendations provided by The Stewart Group regarding Burnaby Capital Project Program Review.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Festivals Grant Program spring 2026 intake
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the spring 2026 intake recommendations for the Festivals Burnaby Grant Program. If approved, this program would provide funding to local festival organizers in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare your festival grant application for the spring 2026 intake.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
ECC - FESTIVALS BURNABY GRANT PROGRAM - SPRING 2026 INTAKE
Purpose: To seek approval for Festivals Burnaby Grant Program, spring intake recommendations.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $1,000 grant to Tsleil-Waututh Nation Giving Circles
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a $1,000 grant to the Tsleil-Waututh Nation Community Society to support their Giving Circles program. This is a proposed donation request on the upcoming council agenda — it has not yet been approved or voted on. If approved, the money would go toward community giving initiatives run by the Tsleil-Waututh Nation.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
ECC - TSLEL-WAUTUTH NATION COMMUNITY SOCIETY - GIVING CIRCLES DONATION REQUEST
Purpose: To seek approval to provide a grant in the amount of $1,000 to Tsleil-Waututh Nation Community Society to support their Giving Circles.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 32 and 28-storey apartment towers on Silver Avenue, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application for two new apartment towers at 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue. The proposal would allow a 32-storey and a 28-storey building in the Metrotown Downtown Plan area. This is a first step — if Council approves forwarding the application, it will go to a public hearing and further readings before any final decision is made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to have a say on the proposed towers.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PDC - REZ #25-12 - 6470 AND 6508 SILVER AVENUE - 32-STOREY AND 28-STOREY APARTMENT BUILDINGS (METROTOWN DOWNTOWN PLAN)
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-12 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mixed-use high-rise rezoning on Dow Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-23) for a mixed-use high-rise development at 6645, 6659, 6675, 6691, and 6707 Dow Avenue to First and Second Reading. This is an early step in the approval process — if Council authorizes it, the proposal will move to a formal reading stage at a future meeting. The project is part of the Metrotown Downtown Plan and would allow a high-rise building with both residential and commercial uses. No final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if the rezoning moves forward, you may want to attend or submit comments.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
PDC - REZ #25-23 - 6645, 6659, 6675, 6691 AND 6707 DOW AVENUE - MIXED-USE HIGH-RISE DEVELOPMENT (METROTOWN DOWNTOWN PLAN)
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-23 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: City to take over management of 6 townhouses at 4878 Brentwood
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a plan for the City to directly manage six non-market townhouses at 4878 Brentwood Drive. The current operator, Metro Vancouver Housing Corporation (MVHC), has chosen not to renew its agreement. If approved, the City would take over tenancy management after the lease ends on June 30, 2026. This is a proposed change — it has not been finalized yet.

key_dates.label: City assumes management of townhouses at 4878 Brentwood — lease expiry

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
4878 BRENTWOOD DRIVE - TRANSITION OF TENANCY MANAGEMENT
Purpose: To inform Council that Metro Vancouver Housing Corporation (MVHC) has elected not to renew its agreement to operate the six City‑owned non‑market townhouses at 4878 Brentwood Drive, and that the City will assume direct management of the properties following lease expiry on June 30, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider: Building permit activity report for April 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will review a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from April 1 to 30, 2026. This report gives council information on current construction activity in the city. It is for information only — no new rules or fees are being proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 04 FROM APRIL 1 - APRIL 30, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey apartment rezoning at 5035 Beresford St
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would rezone 5035 Beresford Street to allow a 20-storey apartment building with 215 residential units and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the city's 2050 Official Community Plan (the long-term land-use blueprint). This is only the first reading — the decision is not final, and further public input may be required before approval.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14820 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 10, 2026 - REZ #25-11 (5035 Beresford St.)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 20-storey multiple-family apartment development comprised of 215 residential units above undeground parking. Th proposal is consistent with the 2050 OCP land use designation for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a new Development Procedures Bylaw (Bylaw 2026) that would change how the city reviews and approves development applications. This proposed bylaw would set out the rules and steps developers and property owners must follow when applying to build or redevelop land in Burnaby. Council will consider giving the bylaw first, second, and third reading at an upcoming meeting — these are the formal steps needed before the bylaw can be adopted and take effect. If approved, the new rules would replace the current development procedures.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if you plan to submit a development application in Burnaby, the new rules could change your process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14824 - Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to implement procedures for review and approval of development applications

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend building bylaw for development procedures
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016. This amendment, called Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026, would change the rules and procedures for how development applications are handled in the city. If approved, it could affect how you submit building plans, get permits, and follow city requirements for new construction or renovations. The exact changes are not yet public, but the purpose is to update the development process. Business owners in construction and property development should watch for the council decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the full amendment details once published — this could change how you apply for building permits.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14825 - Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016 (development procedures)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on fee changes for development procedures
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the fees and charges for development procedures in Burnaby. The proposed bylaw, called 'Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026', would update the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, this could affect the cost of permits, applications, or other steps needed for construction or development projects. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — proposed fee changes could affect your development project costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14826 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (development procedures)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Agreement for non-market rentals at Newcombe Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the rental rates and ensures the units stay affordable for the long term. If approved, this agreement would guarantee that the new rental homes remain non-market (below market price) and available to people with lower incomes. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — watch for the council decision if you are a developer or landlord interested in similar agreements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14827 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (7880 & 7885 Newcombe Street) Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement for the non-market rental housing development at 7880 & 7885 Newcombe Street

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Agreement for non-market rental at Marine Drive
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the rental rates and ensures the units stay affordable for the long term. If approved, this agreement would create new below-market rental homes in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect rental housing supply and competition in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.5
#14828 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive) Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement for the non-market rental housing development at 6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 40-storey mixed-use building rezoning at 5978 Wilson Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would rezone 5978 Wilson Avenue. If approved, the change would allow a developer to build a 40-storey mixed-use building. The project would include market and non-market rental apartments, a child care facility, and a small commercial space for neighbourhood businesses. This is a rezoning application — a request to change the official zoning rules for that specific property. The decision has not been made yet; Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak for or against the project before Council votes.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Child Care
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14759 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 12, 2025 - REZ #24-22 (5978 Wilson Ave)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a high-rise residential development with a neighbourhood commercial unit comprised of a 40-storey mixed-use building which includes a child care facility and non-market and market rental residential units

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Master Plan amendment for 6800 Lougheed Hwy in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would update the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway. The amendment would establish a new site plan with updated building heights and massing (the overall shape and size of buildings) to match the city's new Official Community Plan (OCP 2050) and its new height-based zoning districts (R6 and CM1). It would also expand open space on the site. No actual development or subdivision is being proposed at this time — this is only a plan-level change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this plan change could affect future development potential on this site.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.2
#14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026 - REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy)
Purpose: to advance a Master Plan (MP) amendment to the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan to establish a revised site plan with updated height and massing in alignment with the recently adopted OCP 2050 and associated height-based zoning districts (R6 and CM1), as well as expanded open space provisions for the site. No development or subdivision is proposed through the subject MP amendment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Master plan amendment for 4567 Lougheed Hwy in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would update the master plan for the Brentwood area at 4567 Lougheed Highway. The amendment would change the site plan to include new rules for building heights and massing (how big and bulky buildings can be). It would also add city policies for non-market housing (affordable housing not sold for profit), child care spaces, and parks. No actual construction or land subdivision is being proposed at this time — this is just a planning change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect future development opportunities at this site.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.4.1
#14779 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 16, 2025 - REZ #23-15 (Ptn. of 4567 Lougheed Hwy)
Purpose: to advance a master plan amendment to the Brentwood Site Conceptual Master Plan to incorporate City policies related to non-market housing, child care, and parks and open spaces. Specifically, the amendment proposes to establish a revised site plan with updated height and massing considerations in alignment with the height-based development framework, existing Official Community Plan (OCP), and draft OCP 2050 land use designations. No development or subdivision is proposed concurrent with the Master Plan Amendment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would create new rules to protect tenants who are forced to move because their building is being redeveloped (torn down or renovated). If approved, the bylaw could require landlords to give tenants more notice, provide compensation, or offer the right to return to a new unit at a similar rent. Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting — it is not yet law.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you are a landlord or developer affected by redevelopment rules.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.4.2
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This change is meant to strengthen tenant protection. If approved, the amendment would update how the city enforces rules related to tenants' rights. The exact details of the new protections are not yet public, but the purpose is to give the city more tools to enforce existing tenant protection rules. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments to have your say on this proposed tenant protection bylaw.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.4.3
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby mid-year fee changes up for final adoption
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of mid-year amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, this bylaw would update various fees and charges the city collects from businesses and residents. The changes are proposed to take effect after council votes. Business owners should watch for the outcome, as it could affect licence, permit, or service fees they pay to the city.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your city licence, permit, or service fees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.4.4
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)
